nativeRange.habitat: Generally found in lagoons among algal or seagrass beds and on sand or silt bottoms, frequently in bays or estuaries; depth range reported from roughly 3 to 91 m, most often between 35 and 90 m.

decorPreferences: deep fine sand or silt substrate
open substrate footprint with little to no rockwork
mature live sand bed with established copepod and amphipod populations
scattered seagrass or macroalgae for cover

diet.notes: In the wild, has been described as an opportunistic feeder that picks epifaunal and interstitial invertebrates — mainly small crustaceans, polychaete worms and other meiofauna — from the sediment-water interface. In captivity, generally accepts only live foods on arrival (live enriched brine shrimp, live copepods, live mysis), and is widely reported to refuse prepared foods. Some specimens may transition to frozen mysis once well established, but multiple small feedings per day on or near the substrate are usually required. Starvation is a common cause of loss.

avoidWith: stinging or hitchhiking invertebrates that may harm slow bottom-dwellers

summary: A tiny, bottom-dwelling Indo-Pacific sea moth (family Pegasidae) with a flattened, armoured body and wing-like pectoral fins. Generally considered an expert-only species due to its near-exclusive reliance on live foods and slow, methodical feeding habit.
careNotes: Best housed in a mature, peaceful species-style aquarium with a deep fine sand or silt substrate, little to no rockwork, and an established population of copepods and amphipods — the species is a slow, methodical bottom-feeder that picks meiofauna and small live prey directly off the sediment and is generally outcompeted by faster tankmates. Reef-safe toward corals and most sessile invertebrates. Tank-size recommendations across sources varied (Quality Marine ~30 US gal / 113 L, LiveAquaria 50 US gal / ~189 L, Reeflex ~200 L); given the species' need for substantial open substrate footprint and the well-documented difficulty of keeping it fed, a 200 L tank with a long, low footprint was chosen. Adult-size figures also varied widely (FishBase 10 cm TL, Reeflex ~7 cm, Quality Marine ~3 in / ~8 cm, LiveAquaria up to 7 in / ~17.8 cm); the FishBase / Reeflex / Quality Marine consensus of roughly 7–10 cm was adopted, and LiveAquaria's 7-inch figure is considered an outlier inconsistent with the type description. Wild adults tend to occur in obligately monogamous pairs and have been observed periodically shedding their skin in one piece with a rapid jump — likely a parasite-removal behaviour. Generally advised to be kept as a male–female pair rather than singly, ideally in a species-only or seahorse/pipefish/dragonet display where slow feeding is accommodated. Care level is widely described as expert; wild-caught only, and shipping survival has been reported as poor.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to form obligately monogamous pair bonds in the wild, with spawning pairs rising roughly 50 cm above the substrate, pressing their ventral surfaces together and releasing gametes at the apex. Captive breeding in home aquaria has not been meaningfully documented and is generally considered impractical; offspring are not available in the trade.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Care level (Expert Only), peaceful temperament, reef-safe status, water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), 50 US gal minimum tank, diet (amphipods, copepods, isopods, live brine and mysis), and recommended tankmates (seahorses, pipefishes, gobies, dragonets). Stated adult size of 7 in / ~17.8 cm treated as an outlier vs. FishBase / Reeflex / Quality Marine consensus and not adopted.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Pegasidae, Dactylopteriformes), maximum length 10 cm TL, preferred temperature 21.8–28.3°C, depth range 3–91 m (mostly 35–90 m), Indo-Pacific distribution from the Red Sea and South Africa to the Marquesas and Society Islands, demersal lagoon/sand/silt habitat, obligate monogamy with close-pair bonding, and the periodic single-piece skin-shedding behaviour.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (~7 cm), recommended tank size (~200 L / 44 US gal), temperature range (22–28°C), Indo-Pacific origin, expert-only difficulty rating, and confirmation that the species is wild-caught only (not bred in captivity).

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Cross-checked Pegasidae classification, adult size (~3+ in / ~8 cm), 30 US gal minimum tank, 72–78°F / pH 8.1–8.4 / SG 1.020–1.025 water parameters, exclusively-live diet (live brine, copepods, fed 5–10× daily), peaceful temperament with slow-feeding tankmate requirement, and the warning that the species ships poorly and is rarely seen in the trade.

fish.breedingNotes: Wild spawning involves obligately monogamous pairs rising above the substrate to release gametes. Captive breeding in home aquaria is not meaningfully documented.
